description: The Anuradhapura Vivekananda Society Limited Petitioner Vs. The Divisional Secretary of Nuwaragam Palatha East Anuradhapura and Others View More before Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: The Anuradhapura Vivekananda Society Limited Petitioner Vs. The Divisional Secretary of Nuwaragam Palatha East Anuradhapura and Others The Anuradhapura Vivekananda Society Limited Petitioner Vs. The Divisional Secretary of Nuwaragam Palatha East Anuradhapura and Others View More
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner, a Society running a religious institute and Hindu Temple, was occupying a land within the vicinity of the Sri Maha Bodhi in the sacred city of Anuradhapura. Later, with the dawn of the Buddha Jayanthi era of 1956, the Petitioner was relocated by giving a land, where the Petitioner also ran a school which was vested in the Government under the Assisted Schools and Training Colleges Act. Thereafter, the Petitioner was placed in possession of another land, where it operated a religious institute including a Temple, cultural centre, and restaurant. The excess land was blocked out and given to the 8th to 10th Respondents. Dispute arose between them with regard to the regularisation and legalisation of their possession by the Government on the recommendation of the Provincial Land Commissioner, which the Petitioner objected to. Held - Application dismissed on the basis that the Petitioner has no legal title to claim nor any legitimate expectation to claim any title document since the Petitioner has failed to improve or develop the entire land; the Petitioner is not entitled to benefit out of his own fault.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka
